# OpenClaw x Discord Server Integration Guide. Architecture diagram of OpenClaw integrated with Discord server. After embedding an OpenClaw AI agent into a Discord server, your community gains the following capabilities:. Compared to Telegram's personal remote control scenario or Slack's enterprise workflow scenario, the Discord x OpenClaw integration is best suited for **community management, open-source project support, and large-scale multi-user interaction**. Step 1: Create a Discord Bot and Obtain Token. This is the foundation of the entire **OpenClaw Discord setup** process -- creating an Application and Bot on the Discord Developer Portal and obtaining the necessary authentication Token. ### 3.1 Create a Discord Application. ### 3.2 Create Bot and Obtain Token. **Important security reminder:** The Bot Token is equivalent to full access credentials for the Bot -- anyone who has it can control your Bot. Never expose the Token in public places (GitHub, Discord channels, screenshots). After obtaining the Discord Bot Token, the next step is to write it into OpenClaw's configuration so the Gateway can connect to Discord as your Bot.

# Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack? Best chat app for OpenClaw compared: Telegram wins for most users, Discord for power users, Slack for teams. Here is how to pick the right one. ## Which OpenClaw channel is right for you? OpenClaw (previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot) connects to Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal, WhatsApp, and several other platforms through a single gateway process. | Team already on Slack | Slack | Don't move your team for this |. For running multiple channels at once (say, Telegram for personal use and Discord for a team), see OpenClaw multi-agent and multi-channel setup guide. Telegram is the fastest, most stable path to a working OpenClaw setup. It uses the official Telegram Bot API via grammY, runs on long-polling by default, and doesn't need a public IP address or a webhook server. Full setup: OpenClaw Telegram Setup Guide. Full setup: OpenClaw Discord Setup Guide.

How to set up OpenClaw across WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack - How to set up OpenClaw across WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord and Slack. Multi-channel is the feature that makes OpenClaw feel “everywhere” without turning into a mess of separate bots. You run one OpenClaw instance on a server or home box. Now you can talk to the same assistant from WhatsApp on your phone, Telegram on your laptop, Discord with your team, and Slack at work. If you’re new to OpenClaw, read what OpenClaw is and how it works first. This article is focused on the “many inboxes” part and how to set it up without shooting yourself in the foot. So if you start a conversation on WhatsApp and continue later on Telegram, OpenClaw can keep the thread because the context is shared. OpenClaw’s WhatsApp integration is based on the WhatsApp Web protocol.

Technology Encyclopedia Home >How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. # How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. The fastest path is not DIY. It is a clean template and a tight onboarding loop. is a practical question — let’s answer it in a way you can deploy. Channels act as the interface, and you can configure popular messaging integrations via the template panel or the onboarding wizard depending on the channel. ## The practical deployment path (Lighthouse). If you want a clean, reproducible setup, the most efficient route is the OpenClaw application template on Tencent Cloud Lighthouse. Even if the template handles day-one setup, you still need a repeatable way to configure models, connect channels, install skills, and keep the gateway alive. When you are ready to run it 24/7 in a clean, isolated environment, start here again: Tencent Cloud Lighthouse Special Offer. * Confirm the agent is running as a background service (not tied to your SSH session).

title: 🦞 How to Use Cron Jobs in OpenClaw to Automate Nightly Tasks Without Hitting Rate Limits | MindStudio
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# How to Use Cron Jobs in OpenClaw to Automate Nightly Tasks Without Hitting Rate Limits. Scheduling OpenClaw crons overnight preserves your rolling quota window for daytime use. Here's how to set up nightly automation with smart scheduling. If you run OpenClaw automations during working hours, you’ve probably run into this: requests start failing mid-workflow, dashboards fill with error logs, and workflows stall right when you need them most. The fix isn’t a bigger plan — it’s scheduling cron jobs to run overnight, when your rolling quota window is clear. Shift your heavy workloads to 2–3 AM and those requests expire from the window before your team arrives. This guide walks through how to set up nightly cron jobs in OpenClaw, schedule them intelligently, and build in the error handling that overnight automation requires. With a rolling window, if your limit is 1,000 requests per hour and you made 400 requests at 9:30 AM, those 400 requests stop counting at 10:30 AM — not at 10:00 AM.

title: 🦞 Scheduled tasks - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# List all jobsopenclaw cron list # Get one stored job as JSONopenclaw cron get <jobId> # Show one job, including resolved delivery routeopenclaw cron show <jobId> # Edit a jobopenclaw cron edit <jobId> --message "Updated prompt" --model "opus" # Force run a job nowopenclaw cron run <jobId> # Force run a job now and wait for its terminal statusopenclaw cron run <jobId> --wait --wait-timeout 10m --poll-interval 2s # Run only if dueopenclaw cron run <jobId> --due # View run historyopenclaw cron runs --id <jobId> --limit 50 # View one exact runopenclaw cron runs --id <jobId> --run-id <runId> # Delete a jobopenclaw cron remove <jobId> # Agent selection (multi-agent setups)openclaw cron create "0 6 * * *" "Check ops queue" --name "Ops sweep" --session isolated --agent opsopenclaw cron edit <jobId> --clear-agent. title: 🦞 Cron - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Cron. # `openclaw cron`. Manage cron jobs for the Gateway scheduler. Run `openclaw cron --help` for the full command surface. `openclaw cron create` is an alias for `openclaw cron add`. Use `--webhook <url>` when the job should POST the finished payload instead of delivering to a chat target:. Use `--command` for deterministic shell-style jobs that should run inside OpenClaw cron without starting an isolated agent/model run:. Command jobs capture stdout/stderr, record normal cron history, and route output through the same `announce`, `webhook`, or `none` delivery modes as isolated jobs. `openclaw cron list` and `openclaw cron show <job-id>` preview the resolved delivery route. Isolated `cron add` jobs default to `--announce` delivery. Isolated cron chat delivery is shared between the agent and the runner:. Use `cron add|create --webhook <url>` or `cron edit <job-id> --webhook <url>` to set webhook delivery. `cron edit <job-id> --webhook <url>`. `openclaw cron run <job-id>`. `openclaw cron run <job-id>openclaw cron runs --id <job-id> --run-id <run-id>`.

title: 🦞 Job Management - OpenClaw Automation & Workflows | Stanza
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Once you have created cron jobs, you need to manage them: list active jobs, edit schedules, enable or disable jobs temporarily, review execution history, and trigger immediate runs for testing. OpenClaw provides a complete set of CLI commands for every management operation. After the incident is resolved, they re-enable the jobs. The next morning, they review the execution history to confirm everything resumed correctly. The `cron list` command shows all configured jobs:. This outputs a table with each job's ID, schedule, status (enabled/disabled), last run time, and next scheduled execution. The `cron edit` command modifies an existing job without recreating it:. `openclaw cron edit nightly-review \. This changes the nightly-review job to run at 3 AM instead of 2 AM and switches its model. openclaw cron enable nightly-review`. The `cron runs` command shows past executions for a specific job:. `openclaw cron runs nightly-review --limit 10`. `openclaw cron run nightly-review`. The immediate run is recorded in the execution history alongside scheduled runs.
